classObjective: Analyze properties preserved under rotations and identify common errors in reasoning about rotations.
workedExamples: 13-3, Example 3 — Rotate a Quadrilateral 90° and Analyze Preserved Properties
13-3, Example 4 — Apply Rotation to a Real-World Scale Drawing
13-3, Example 5 — Identify Common Errors in Rotation Reasoning
warmUp: What properties (distance, angle measure, orientation) are preserved under rotation?
conceptDevelopment: Mini-lesson: properties preserved under rotations; 90°, 180°, 270° preserving distance from center; common rotation errors
guidedPractice: Teacher-led practice analyzing preserved properties and identifying rotation errors
independentPractice: Practice problems analyzing rotation properties and solving rotation problems
assessment: Exit ticket: explain why a 90° rotation about the origin preserves distance from the origin and identify a common rotation error
capReflection: What happens to the orientation of a figure when you rotate it? How is this different from reflection?
